summary: Iteration 3 of design-writer/output/design.md remains fully aligned with both guides. The only changes since the passing iteration-2 review are additive UI: a new `define_business_requirements` helper and inline 'Requirements' block (§6.2/§6.3, F1), and widening the Ask Human anchor to `questions.present? || phase.paused?` with an empty-state form (§6.3, F2). Both were verified against the actual repo. Backend (guides/backend-guide.md): `define_business_requirements` delegates to the same `define_artifact` reader as the existing `define_open_questions`/`define_discovery_notes` — a presentation-only artifact read in a helper, no business logic ('business logic lives in reusable POROs'); the whole service/query seam is unchanged from iteration 2 — verb-first `self.call` -> Result services with keyword args, service-owned transactions, after-commit broadcasts, thin PRG controllers reusing `membership_scoped_phase`, `Phases::Convergence` in app/queries/ (dir confirmed present) as a read-only predicate, model gains only the `paused` enum value plus the `any_step_active?` predicate with no business-logic callbacks, and Minitest coverage is listed for every new/changed service, query, controller, model, and helper. UI (guides/ui-style-guide.md): the new 'Requirements' block mirrors the discovery-notes block exactly (`mt-6 border-t border-gray-100 pt-6`, `text-sm font-semibold text-gray-900` heading, `text-sm text-gray-700` body) using allowed spacing steps and one source of truth; the Ask Human form matches the 'Forms' spec precisely (label `text-sm font-medium`, help `text-xs text-gray-500`, input `rounded-md ring-1 ring-inset ring-gray-300 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-600`, primary submit `bg-indigo-600 hover:bg-indigo-700`, `data-turbo-submits-with` for disable-on-submit); the empty state is a sentence followed by the form's primary action, not a bare empty region; `paused` maps to the amber 'attention' tone in the single shared STATUS_TONES map (verified in status_helper.rb) with status_badge always rendering the label, never color alone; progress messages use aria-live="polite". Non-blocking observation (not a finding, pre-existing and consistent): in-card sub-headings ('Discovery notes', 'Requirements', 'Open questions') use `text-sm font-semibold`, which isn't a verbatim entry in the type scale, but this exactly matches the partial's established sub-heading precedent that passed in iterations 1-2 and is internally consistent, so it is not a new deviation.
